name: Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania (Germany)

colors.symbolism: from the historic colours of Mecklenburg
from the historic colours of Pomerania
from the historic colours of Mecklenburg
from the historic colours of Mecklenburg and Pomerania

description: Five horizontal stripes: a wide ultramarine blue at the top, a white band, a narrow yellow band, another white band, and a wide red at the bottom.
history: Adopted on 29 January 1991 by the re-established Land of Mecklenburg-Western Pomerania after German reunification. The civil flag uses only the colour stripes; the state flag adds the state coat of arms in the centre.

features.symbolism: Combines the historic colours of Mecklenburg (blue, yellow, red) with the white-and-red of Pomerania, brought together to represent the merged northern Land formed in 1990.
